title: M6 Northbound: Lane Closure near Stafford
evidence: National Highways NTIS reports lane 2 closure on M6 northbound between J13 and J14 near Stafford. Minor carriageway debris following overnight winds. Traffic moving slowly but no significant queuing. Routine maintenance crew deployed.

assessment.justification: Single-source automated sensor report. No corroboration from other channels. Routine incident with no connection to the developing meteorological situation in the west.
assessment.synthesis: Routine lane closure on M6 northbound due to carriageway debris near Stafford. No significant disruption anticipated. Geographically dispersed and unrelated to the developing weather pattern.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Single automated sensor report. No contextual relevance to developing situation.

title: A1(M) Southbound: Congestion and Reduced Visibility near Stevenage
evidence: National Highways traffic management centre reports congestion on A1(M) southbound near J7 Stevenage. Reduced visibility logged by operators. No closures in place. Traffic building through evening peak. Standard advisory issued.

assessment.justification: Routine evening congestion on A1(M). Reduced visibility may be associated with early frontal cloud but no direct connection to the developing weather system is established. Dispersed from area of primary interest.
assessment.synthesis: Routine congestion on A1(M) southbound during evening peak near Stevenage. Peripheral and dispersed from the primary area of interest. Baseline noise only.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Routine incident with no clear link to the developing meteorological event.

title: A40 Eastbound: Surface Water Accumulation near Raglan
evidence: Traffic Wales CCTV operators report surface water accumulation on A40 eastbound approaching Raglan junction. Advisory speed restriction in place. Light to moderate rain falling. No closures at this stage. Conditions being monitored.

assessment.justification: Traffic Wales CCTV observation confirmed. Surface water consistent with rain onset in Monmouthshire. Early indicator of developing conditions on the A40 corridor, a key route between Southeast Wales and the English Midlands.
assessment.synthesis: Surface water accumulating on A40 near Raglan in the early hours of Friday. First road-related signal from the Southeast Wales area of primary interest. Conditions likely to develop as rainfall continues.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Early-stage CCTV observation. Conditions evolving.

title: M4 Westbound: Reduced Speeds and Surface Water near Newport J28
evidence: Traffic Wales NTIS sensors record significant speed reduction on M4 westbound between J28 and J27 Newport. Spray and standing water reported by operators. Variable message signs activated with reduced speed limit of 50mph.

assessment.justification: Automated speed sensors corroborated by CCTV operator observation. Rainfall intensifying in Newport area during early hours. Pattern consistent with the approaching frontal system.
assessment.synthesis: Early-morning speed reduction on M4 westbound near Newport reflects onset of rainfall on this critical England–Wales corridor. First M4 signal of the event. Conditions developing.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Automated sensor data only. Conditions developing.

title: A470 Northbound: Surface Water and Poor Visibility near Brecon
evidence: Traffic Wales advisory for surface water on A470 northbound approaching Brecon from the south. Reduced visibility in valley sections. HGV advisory in place. Conditions deteriorating slowly.

assessment.justification: Single Traffic Wales advisory consistent with rain onset in the Brecon Beacons upland area. No corroborating reports at this stage. Relevant as an upstream catchment indicator.
assessment.synthesis: Surface water and reduced visibility on A470 near Brecon. Early-stage impact in the upland catchment area north of the primary impact zone. Hydrologically significant given the Usk and Wye headwaters in this area.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Single advisory. Early stage.

title: M5 Southbound: Heavy Rain and Standing Water near J24 Bridgwater
evidence: National Highways reports standing water on M5 southbound between J24 and J25 near Bridgwater. Variable message signs deployed. Traffic flowing with caution. Rainfall rate increasing. No closures in place.

assessment.justification: National Highways NTIS data confirmed. Rainfall reaching Somerset ahead of frontal passage. Consistent with the approaching system tracking northeast. Part of broader Southwest England pattern.
assessment.synthesis: Standing water developing on M5 near Bridgwater in the early morning. Part of a broad pattern of minor road impacts emerging across Southwest England and Southeast Wales as the weather system advances.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Early-stage impacts across a wide area. Pattern emerging.

evidence: Traffic Wales reports surface water on A48 westbound between Chepstow and Caerwent. Spray reducing visibility. Advisory speed limit in place. No closures. Rainfall persisting.

assessment.synthesis: Surface water advisory on A48 near Chepstow. Second Monmouthshire road impact of the early morning, reinforcing the emerging geographic focus on Southeast Wales.

evidence: National Highways reports slow-moving traffic on M5 northbound near J28 due to heavy rainfall and spray. CCTV confirms standing water in nearside lane. Traffic building as early commuters encounter conditions. No closures.

assessment.justification: Automated sensor and CCTV corroboration for M5 J28 near Cullompton. Rainfall affecting Devon ahead of the main frontal passage. Consistent with broader Southwest England pattern.
assessment.synthesis: M5 northbound impacted by heavy rain near Cullompton, Devon. Peripheral impact in Southwest England consistent with the developing weather event moving northeast.

evidence: National Highways reports poor visibility on A30 westbound due to heavy spray near Bodmin Moor. Speed restrictions in place. Traffic lighter than usual at this hour. Conditions expected to ease as front moves northeast.

title: A483 Southbound: Surface Water near Builth Wells
evidence: Traffic Wales advisory for A483 southbound near Builth Wells. Surface water in low-lying sections. River Wye level rising in the adjacent valley. Conditions being monitored by regional teams.

assessment.justification: Traffic Wales advisory with notation of rising river levels. River Wye response time from Powys catchment rainfall is hydrologically relevant to downstream conditions at Hereford and Ross-on-Wye. Early upstream indicator.
assessment.synthesis: Surface water on A483 near Builth Wells with river levels beginning to rise in the Wye catchment. Early upstream signal with downstream hydrological implications for Herefordshire.

title: A465 Eastbound: Road Closed – Severe Flooding near Brynmawr
locationName: Blaenau Gwent | A465 Heads of the Valleys | Blaenau Gwent
evidence: Traffic Wales confirms full closure of A465 eastbound between Brynmawr and Clydach due to severe flooding. Flood water at least 60cm deep across carriageway. Emergency diversion via A4047 in place. Multiple calls to emergency services. Environment Agency flood warning active for Clydach Gorge. No re-opening time given.

assessment.justification: Traffic Wales has confirmed full closure. Environment Agency flood warning active for Clydach Gorge. Multiple corroborating reports from emergency services and local social media. Flood depth of 60cm+ presents risk to all vehicle types.
assessment.synthesis: The A465 Heads of the Valleys Road is fully closed east of Brynmawr due to severe flooding in the Clydach Gorge. This is a strategic east-west arterial route for Southeast Wales and its closure represents a major disruption to regional connectivity. No timeline for re-opening has been given and conditions continue to deteriorate.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Full road closure confirmed by Traffic Wales. Active Environment Agency flood warning. Multiple corroborating sources.

title: M4 Eastbound: Full Closure between J24 and J25 Newport
evidence: Traffic Wales confirms M4 eastbound fully closed between J24 Coldra and J25 Caerleon following severe standing water and structural concerns on the carriageway. Flood depth exceeding 1 metre in the central section. All traffic diverted via A48. Major queuing developing westbound from J25. South Wales Police and Highways Wales on scene. Structural assessment underway.

assessment.justification: Full closure confirmed by Traffic Wales and South Wales Police. Flood depth exceeds safe vehicle traversal threshold. Structural assessment initiated. M4 closure cuts the primary road link between England and South Wales, representing the most significant road impact of the event.
assessment.synthesis: The M4 motorway is fully closed between J24 and J25 east of Newport, severing the primary road link between England and South Wales. Confirmed flood water depths exceed 1 metre on the carriageway. The closure is causing major knock-on disruption as traffic diverts onto the A48 and local routes, many of which are also impacted by flooding.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Full motorway closure confirmed. Structural assessment ongoing. Cross-border strategic impact.

title: A40 Westbound: Partial Closure and Flooding near Abergavenny
evidence: Traffic Wales reports partial closure of A40 westbound approaching Abergavenny. Flood water across nearside lane, advisory speed limit in place for remaining lane. River Usk tributary overtopping nearby. Emergency services on standby. Conditions deteriorating.

assessment.justification: Traffic Wales confirmation of partial lane closure. Flood water visible on CCTV. River Usk tributary response consistent with accumulated rainfall in the Usk catchment. Conditions assessed as likely to worsen.
assessment.synthesis: A40 westbound partially blocked by flooding west of Abergavenny. This strategic route connecting the Vale of Glamorgan to Brecon and Herefordshire is under increasing pressure. River levels rising rapidly. Full closure assessed as a likely outcome if conditions continue.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Partial closure confirmed. Conditions developing. Full closure risk elevated.

title: A449 Northbound: Flooding and Lane Closures between Raglan and Monmouth
evidence: Traffic Wales reports flooding on A449 northbound between Raglan and Monmouth. Lane 2 closed. Emergency vehicles attending. Flood water from adjacent farmland overtopping drainage. Significant queuing developing southbound from Monmouth.

assessment.justification: Traffic Wales lane closure confirmed and corroborated by emergency vehicle attendance. Flooding from Raglan fields is well-documented in this event type. Monmouth community reports on social media consistent with official data. High-confidence closure at a strategically important junction.
assessment.synthesis: A449 northbound severely disrupted by flooding between Raglan and Monmouth. Multiple corroborating sources including emergency services attendance and social media reports from Monmouth town. Critical route now failing as diversionary capacity continues to erode.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Official closure confirmed by Traffic Wales and corroborated by emergency services and community reporting.

title: A417 Northbound: Serious Surface Water near Cirencester
evidence: National Highways reports significant surface water accumulation on A417 northbound near Cirencester. Standing water across two lanes. Traffic reduced to single file. Vehicle recovery ongoing after HGV aquaplaned. Variable message signs activated on approach from M4 J15.

assessment.justification: National Highways confirms two-lane obstruction. HGV aquaplaning incident corroborates severity of surface water. A417 disruption near Cirencester affects traffic diverting north from the M4 corridor, adding to regional impact.
assessment.synthesis: Significant surface water on A417 near Cirencester disrupts a key Gloucestershire arterial route. The incident reflects the spreading impact into northern Gloucestershire and is particularly significant for traffic seeking alternatives to the M4 closure.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Multi-lane obstruction confirmed. Corroborated by vehicle recovery incident.

title: A40 Eastbound: Road Closed – Severe Wye Flooding near Ross-on-Wye
evidence: National Highways and Herefordshire Highways report flooding on A40 eastbound approaching Ross-on-Wye from the west. River Wye at 4.2m and rising at the Ross gauge. Flood water encroaching on carriageway. Single-lane flow being managed. Risk of full closure assessed as significant.

assessment.justification: Full closure confirmed by National Highways and Herefordshire Highways. EA gauge at Ross-on-Wye showing 4.2m and rising strongly corroborates closure. Multiple agency corroboration from official sources with real-time gauge data. Hereford Times and community reports further confirm.
assessment.synthesis: A40 fully closed by severe River Wye flooding at Ross-on-Wye. EA gauge at 4.2m and rising with peak not yet reached. Multiple official agency corroboration. This closure cuts the primary remaining through-route from SE Wales and Herefordshire towards the M50 and English Midlands.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Full closure confirmed by two highways authorities. Active EA flood warning. River gauge corroboration.
